metadata.notes_on_baseline: Baselines not re-run in iteration 2 — reuse iteration 1 baselines as the comparison anchor since they are skill-independent.

notes: Again caught + fixed the sibling areaServed='Duesseldorf' clone-bug.
Placed Bottrop between Bochum and Wuppertal (logical Ruhrgebiet adjacency) vs iter 1's placement after Oberhausen — both valid.
Page inherits site's --accent-2 steel for h2 em-text (correct per updated tokens posture).
Explicitly cites the new calibration anchor from updated SKILL.md: 'BILDER, DIE EINEN RAUM VERÄNDERN.'
Uses var(--accent-2) for the headline em — implementing the steel/sage accent system explicitly.
Adds a 'treatment' select (monochrome default + original opt-out) — a useful generalization over iter 1.
Anti-pattern audit preserved: 'Not a trusted by sponsor wall.'
CTAs now use btn-ink + btn-ghost — NO red-filled btn-accent (iter 1 had this wrong).
Explicit warning in response: 'Roter Hover-Fill auf den CTAs ... nicht zum Site-Rot wechseln.'
Same correct recommendation (Iris straffen statt ersetzen) as iter 1.
Adds mobile-bypass (clip-path:none under 780px) — practical performance fix that wasn't in iter 1.

qualitative_changes_iter1_to_iter2.change: BIG — CTA pattern corrected from btn-accent (red fill, anti-pattern) to btn-ink + btn-ghost (correct). Agent now explicitly warns against switching CTA hover to site-red.
qualitative_changes_iter1_to_iter2.trigger: Updated SKILL.md posture rule #3 (two-tier accent) + 03-hero-formulas.md CTA convention.

qualitative_changes_iter1_to_iter2.change: MODERATE — Now explicitly invokes the 'BILDER, DIE EINEN RAUM VERÄNDERN' calibration anchor from the new SKILL.md section. Uses var(--accent-2) for em — explicit steel.
qualitative_changes_iter1_to_iter2.trigger: New 'Calibration: what the rendered site actually looks like' section in SKILL.md.

qualitative_changes_iter1_to_iter2.change: SMALL — Different city placement in cities grid (Bochum/Wuppertal neighborhood vs after Oberhausen). Both correct. Output mostly unchanged because this task is mechanical.

notes: Iter 2 confirms the user's feedback fixed the most visible failure mode: red-filled CTAs in eval 3 are gone, replaced with ink-fill/ghost.
The new calibration anchor section in SKILL.md is being consumed: eval 2 agent directly quoted 'BILDER, DIE EINEN RAUM VERÄNDERN.'
Mechanical tasks (SEO subpage) are unchanged because they were already correct.
Pass rate is still 100% across all three evals — same as iter 1 — but the *content* of with-skill output is now closer to the rendered site spec.
Tokens slightly down (~5%) — likely the updated SKILL.md being more direct/concrete about anti-patterns means less re-reading and verification.
